original: French King Hugo Capet crowns his son Robert the compassionate king
Pontifical decree "Sancta Romania" against spiritualists
Sweden's original Drottningholm Palace burns down, rebuilt from 1662 in its modern form to design by Nikodemus Tessin
"Messiah" Sjabtai Tswi departs to Constantinople
Abraham Crijnssen departs to Suriname
Baron Karl Rabenhaupt occupies Coevorden, Netherlands
Don Francisco de Agurto installed as land guardian of Southern Netherlands
Henry Purcell & Nahum Tate's opera "Dido & Aeneas" premieres at Josias Priest's girls' school in Chelsea, London
One of the earliest music concerts in North America held at Peter Pelham's Great Room in Boston, Massachusetts
French troops conquer Grave, Netherlands
Wearing masks at balls forbidden in Boston
Danzig surrenders to allied armies
The Treaty of St. Louis is proclaimed.
Lehman Theater in St Petersburg catches fire; 100s die
Opera "Stradella" after being rewritten is produced (Hamburg)
Dinner party held inside life-sized model of an Iguanodon (ornithopod dinosaur), created by Benjamin Waterhouse Hawkins and Sir Richard Owen in South London
Pennsylvania Rock Oil Co, 1st in US, incorporated in NYC
Philadelphia Knights of Labor forms
American Metrological Society forms (NYC) weights, measures & money
Andrassy Note calls for Christian-Muslim religious freedoms
William Morris, Eleanor Marx, and others establish the Socialist League, a revolutionary organization in the UK
Dr Miles V Lynk, physician, publishes 1st Black medical journal
Russia signs military accord with France
Stanley Cup, Granite Rink, Winnipeg, Manitoba: Ernie McLea scores a hat-trick as Montreal Victorias beat Winnipeg Victorias, 6-5; first SC Challenge game played outside of Montreal
Province of Zululand annexed to Natal colony
American Political Science Association founded at New Orleans
Electric arc lamp sets fire to Iroquois Theatre in Chicago, Illinois, leaving 602 dead in one of the deadliest single-building blazes in American history
Former Governor Frank Steunenberg is assassinated near his home in Caldwell, Idaho
All-India Muslim League is founded in Dacca in East Bengal, British India, later lays the foundations for Pakistan
Iran becomes a constitutional monarchy

Abraham Mills' Mills Committee declares baseball was invented in 1839 by future American Civil War general Abner Doubleday in Cooperstown, New York; widely disproven, now known as 'the Doubleday myth'
Stanley Cup, Montreal Arena, Westmount, Quebec: Montreal Wanderers outscore Edmonton HC, 14-9 in 2 game challenge series
Barnes takes 17 wickets vs South Africa (8-56 & 9-103)
Cromarty Harbour, Scotland - British cruiser Natal explodes: 405 die
-32°F (-36°C) in Mountain City, Tennessee (state record)
-37°F (-38°C) in Lewisburg, WV (state record)
Lincoln's Inn in London admits its first female bar student.
NSW score 705 against Victoria, go from 8-475 to 9-701
Chicago Tribune reports the Tigers threw a 4-game series to the White Sox in 1917 to help Chicago win the pennant (never substantiated)
Paul Eliot Green's "In Abraham's Bosom" premieres in NYC
Japan dedicates 1st subway in Orient (route under 2 miles long)
Sigma Gamma Rho Sorority incorporates
-50°F (-46°C) in Bloomfield, Vermont (state record)
Government disallows NSB-membership for civil service
Jack Badcock scores 274 v Victoria, Tasmania's 1st double-ton
New York Rangers and Boston Bruins battle out a 0-0 tie; first of a 37-game overtime undefeated streak for the Bruins, the longest in NHL history; streak spans over 4 years and includes 27 ties
Italian bombers destroy Swedish Red Cross unit in Ethiopia
United Auto Workers stage 1st sit-down strike, at Fisher Body Plant
O'Reilly takes 14-45 (8-23 & 6-22) as NSW crush Qld in 2 days
California's 1st freeway, (Arroyo Seco Parkway), opens
Nazis require Dutch physicians to join Nazi organization
Phillies trade Babe Dahlgren to Pitts for Babe Phelps & cash
King George II of Greece, abdicates his throne, appointing Archbishop Damaskinos of Athens as Regent in 1945
Sergei Prokofjev's 8th Piano sonata, premieres in Moscow
King Michael of Romania, forced by Communists abdicates his throne, sending him into exlie
Romanian republic proclaimed
India recognizes People's Republic of China
"Sixty Minute Man" record single released by The Dominoes (Billboard Song of the Year 1951)
Vietnam, Laos and Cambodia become Independent states within the French Union
"The Roy Rogers Show", American Western television series starring Roy Rogers and Dale Evans debuts on NBC; runs for 6 seasons
Tuskegee Inst reports 1952 as 1st yr in 71 with no lynchings in US
The first NTSC color television sets go on sale for about $1,175 from RCA
French franc devalued
George Washington, 1st ballistic missile sub commissioned
TV game show "Let's Make A Deal," hosted by Monty Hall, debuts on NBC-TV
Beatles' "Hello Goodbye" single goes #1 & stays #1 for 3 weeks
Great Western Forum opens in LA
-48°F (-44°C), Mazama & Winthrop, Washington (state record)
Frank Sinatrafirst records "My Way" with lyrics were written by Paul Anka and based on the French song "Comme d'habitude"
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
The financial cost of the disturbances and riots in Northern Ireland during 1969 and 1970 are today estimated to be £5.5 million
A member of the Irish Republican Army is killed in a premature bomb explosion in Santry, Dublin.
Long Island NHL franchise purchased (NY Islanders)
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Beatles are legally disbanded (4 years after suit was brought)
Constitution of Democratic Republic of Madagascar comes into force

Carter holds 1st news conference by US President in Eastern Europe (Warsaw)
Ohio State dismisses Woody Hayes as its football coach
Rock group, Emerson, Lake & Palmer break up

"Wonderful World of Disney" last performance on NBC-TV
Anthony Shaffer's "Whodunnit" premieres in NYC
England defeat Australia by three runs at cricket MCG
US Assay Office in New York City, NY closes
IBM-PC DOS Version 3.2 released

Australia hang on for draw v New Zealand at MCG, 1 wkt left 17 runs short
Canadian Senate OK's free trade pact; with US

10th United Negro College Fund raises $12,000,000
11th United Negro College Fund raises $10,000,000
Last day of Test Cricket for Michael Whitney
Washington defenseman Al Iafrate becomes first player in NHL history to have his shot officially break 100 miles per hour; records 101.4 mph during the Capitals Superskills competition
Vatican recognizes Israel
Lowest ever UK temperature recorded of -27.2°C iat Altnaharra in the Scottish Highlands, equaling the record set at Braemar, Aberdeenshire on February 11, 1895 and January 10, 1982
United Nations announces that a total of 21 contracts have been approved for the limited Iraqi oil sales under Resolution 986
An abandoned building collapses on NY's 42nd St, no one hurt
In the worst incident in Algeria's insurgency, the Wilaya of Relizane massacres, 400 people are killed from four villages.
Rizal Day Bombings: A series of bombs explode in various places in Metro Manila, Philippines within a span of a few hours, killing 22 and injuring about a hundred.
U.S. Attorney General John Ashcroft recuses himself and his office from the Plame affair.
A fire in the República Cromagnon nightclub in Buenos Aires, Argentina kills 194.
Tropical Storm Zeta forms in the open Atlantic Ocean, tying the record for the latest tropical cyclone ever to form in the North Atlantic basin
Madrid Barajas International Airport is bombed.
19 people are killed by a suicide bomber in Balochistan, Pakistan
9 people are killed and 26 are injured in a tour bus crash off interstate 84, Oregon, US
The opening of Line 6 of the Beijing subway makes it the longest metro network in the world at 442km
15 people are killed in a trolley-bus bomb attack in Voglograd, Russia
NFL head coachMike Shanahanis fired by the Washington Redskins
83rd Orange Bowl: #10 Florida State beats #6 Michigan, 33-32
A ceasefire organised by Russia, Iran and Turkey between Syria's government and opposition groups takes effect
British singer-songwriter Ray Davies, of The Kinks, is awarded a knighthood byQueen Elizabeth II
Last day Indians are able to deposit in banks 500 and 1,000 rupee notes which were scrapped only 50 days earlier in an anti-corruption measure
84th Orange Bowl: #6 Wisconsin beats #11 Miami (Florida), 34-24
86th Orange Bowl: #6 Florida beats #24 Virginia, 36-28
Argentina is the first major Latin American country to legalize abortion (signed into law 14 Jan 2021)
San Antonio assistant Becky Hammon becomes first female to coach an NBA team after Spurs head coach Gregg Popovich is ejected in a 121-107 loss to the LA Lakers
Rare winter wildfire destroys nearly 1000 homes at the base of the Rocky Mountains, Colorado, in suburban neighborhoods between Boulder and Denver
Wars of the Roses: Battle of Wakefield (Northern England), Duke of York is killed and his forces are soundly defeated by the forces of KingHenry VI
Hungarian aristocratElizabeth Báthoryarrested at Csejte Castle on suspicion of killing and torturing hundreds of young girls and women

Filipino nationalistJosé Rizalis executed by firing squad in Manila by the Spanish

Philadelphia merchant John Wanamaker's flagship department store is officially dedicated by US PresidentWilliam Howard Taft; the Florentine style building designed by architect Daniel H. Burnham, with nine of its twelve floors for retail, features a grand court housing the world's largest pipe organ and a large bronze eagle statue which quickly becomes a popular meeting place
John E. Hoover decides to be calledJ. Edgar Hoover

Congress authorizes Kennedy half dollar, in honor ofJohn F. Kennedy

"That Girl" single released byStevie Wonder(Billboard Song of the Year -1982)

Wayne Gretzkyscores 5 goals and an assist in Edmonton's 7-5 win over Philadelphia; gives Gretzky 50 goals in 39 games, the fastest 50 in 50 in NHL history; also sets NHL record with 15-game assist streak
Prime MinisterRobert Mugabeelected President of Zimbabwe
Oliver Northsubpoenas US PresidentsRonald Reaganand George H.W. Bush as defense witnesses for his upcoming trial
Proposed budget cuts byBenjamin Netanyahuspark protests from 250,000 workers who shut down services across Israel.
NFL head coachMike Shanahanis fired by the Denver Broncos

Patrick Mahomesbecomes only the 2nd quarterback in NFL history to throw for 5,000 yards and 50 touchdowns during KC Chiefs' 35-3 win over Oakland Raiders; achieves both marks with 3rd quarter 89-yard TD pass to Demarcus Robinson
"Queen" guitarist and astrophysicistBrian May(75) receives a British knighthood for his service to music and charity
Cristiano Ronaldojoins Saudi Arabian club Al Nassr for the “biggest salary ever in football”, leaving Manchester United after criticizing it publicly
Creation of the USSR is formally proclaimed in Moscow from the Bolshoi Theatre, organized as a union (U) of Russian, Ukrainian, Byelorussian and Transcaucasian Soviet Socialist Republics (SSR).

In an emotional speech to the Canadian ParliamentWinston Churchillstates Britain will never surrender to "Hitler and his Nazi gang" and that "they have asked for total war. Let us make sure they get it". Afterwards Yousef Karsh captures him in his famous photograph, "The Roaring Lion".
